text: Soapbox racing.
It's been around since the 1940s.
Kids, homemade cars and a dream.
All for the love of going downhill faster than the next racer.
Fast forward to now.
And this is the Red Bull Soapbox Race London.
Over 50 teams, 420 metres of downhill chaos,
jumps, obstacles and builds that would blow the 1940s kids' minds.
But here's the thing, those old wooden crates, they're long gone.
What's replaced them is something else entirely.
The builds of today are in a completely different league.
we're talking about powerful suspension, computer aided designs and some seriously smart steering systems.
They have to be under 80 kilograms, be no longer than 3 .5 meters and be powered by nothing more than a push and gravity.
So no engines, no batteries, just straight -up physics.
And yes, this is a soapbox.
I told you.
The teams are judged on creativity, showmanship and speed so not only do they need to look good they need to put on a performance and finish the race quickly.
Among the teams here today one stood out.
Hey, Brooke Sixform, how we all doing?
How we feeling?
Thank you, very good.
Today, honestly, I'm just buzzing, like, first time, we're the youngest here as well, so we're in it to win it.
They've got a metal worker, a driver, two builders and a designer.
It's recording, guys.
What was that?
You're putting the top bit in first.
It's taken them months to get to this stage in an after -school club.
cutting, grinding, welding and riveting in a workshop.
We decided to base the car off a Ferrari F1 1950.
I designed a whole entire 3D model of the whole thing.
What can you tell me about this braking system?
Okay, so it's salvaged from a bike handle, right?
We connected a long cable to the back of the axle.
There's a brake disc in the back.
Then we got the steering system.
What we decided to do, we just had two steel rods connecting to the wheel pivots.
So you can just very easily turn it from here.
The suspension we put on the front because the front's gonna be the first thing that hits the ground once we get off the ramps in the race.
We don't want it to break down midway.
So yeah, suspension should help out a lot with that.
So yeah, hopefully we win.
What can I say?
I was more of the hands -on person, making sure all the metal work pieces are fit together, measured and using tools.
And I'm going in a workshop to actually cut things up.
When everyone pushes you off from the start line, what's going to be going through your mind?
How to reach the end line then?
Because my mission is to reach the finishing line, to ensure I will get the best marks, to be the fastest, to be the best.
Do you feel any pressure?
No, I don't feel pressure.
The pressure is on the wheels.
We are the youngest competitors as you know feature engineers.
Ultimately we're here to win it.
We're gonna teach the old folks how it's really done you know because we're the next generation.
We're here to outclass everyone.
Confident words but first they've got to put a show on.
It's a good score, but the performance was just the start, the real test is about to begin.
The build looks solid so far, suspension's doing its job and everything's holding up.
Then the track hits back.
With help from the team, she was back up and running.
Until the next crash.
But she never gave up.
Nobody is gonna stop me, no Nobody is gonna stop me,
nobody Is gonna stop me, nobody Is gonna stop me,
no After the race, we found the team to get their take on how it went.
Getting 10 points across the board was one thing, but for Nisha to actually get to the finish line is actually another thing.
And getting to that final push to the final point, even though she crashed, getting to that last bit and never giving up is just unbelievable honestly.
So any tweaks for next time?
Making it a little bit lighter for someone who's light like her to steer it properly is just an upgrade that I would do personally.
I think we should level the tyres because I think the tyres are a bit uneven.
I think that impacted the steering wheel a bit.
So next time we shall make sure by the time we come here we are prepared properly and we are ready for anything.
Out of 52 teams they placed 16th overall, 8th for creativity and number 1 for their performance.
Not bad for this young team.
They built something from scratch and went for it and already talking about next time, that's the engineering mindset.
They may not have won but they've definitely made their mark.
